title: Winey Jamaican Cream Bowl
description: A winey, creamy Jamaican dish featuring szechuan peppercorns and banana leaf, served as a meal-prep breakfast option. This recipe combines the flavors of Jamaica with the convenience of a prepped meal.
instructions: In a large skillet, heat the melted coconut oil over medium heat.
Add the grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the szechuan peppercorns and cook for another minute, until toasted.
Add the chopped scallions and cook for 2 minutes, until softened.
In a large bowl, whisk together the coconut milk, allspice, and namul blanch seasoning until well combined.
Add the cooked scallion mixture to the coconut milk mixture and stir until combined.
Season with freshly ground black pepper to taste.
Transfer the mixture to a blender or food processor and blend until smooth.
Pour the mixture into a bowl or individual serving cups.
Cover with banana leaves and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, or overnight.
Serve chilled, garnished with chopped scallions and a drizzle of maple syrup.
Refrigerate leftovers for up to 3 days.
